result.title: This New AI Model Actually Impressed Me
result.description:
This might be the first AI demo in a LONG time that felt like there was actually a big step up in intelligence. 

Thinking Machines Labs just showed off their new “Interaction Models” and some of these demos genuinely feel like the next evolution of AI.

They showed off real-time translation while someone is still speaking and a deep understanding of timing, pauses, posture, and context during a conversation.

I was genuinely impressed.

Here's the link if you want to check out all the demos and read more information: thinkingmachines.ai/blog/interaction-models

#ai #ainews #aimodels #thinkingmachineslabs

result.title: Why I Didn’t Sell My Channel
result.description:
This video went super viral about how private equity is buying up and ruining YouTube channels. 

And the truth is... I HAVE gotten 2 separate offers to buy my YouTube channel/brand. One was from a private equity group and one from a major software company.

But I turned down both of them and tbh I have no plans to sell my channel or any of my platforms. 

I’d much rather keep building and working on what I have than watch it slowly turn into corporatized content slop 🤷🏻‍♂️

#AI #ainews #privateequity #contentcreation #socialmedia

result.title: My Favorite AI Model Right Now
result.description:
I'm always being asked to pick my favorite AI model, but the truth is my favorite changes all the time based on what's out there.

At the moment, GPT-5.5 is probably my go-to for almost everything like coding, brainstorming, and general questions.

But a month ago I probably would’ve said Claude Opus, and before that I really liked Gemini.
Same thing with image models. Right now I’m loving GPT-Images 2.0, especially for prompts with text. But Nano Banana 2 was the frontrunner for a while.

Curious what everyone else is using right now. If you could only choose ONE AI model to keep, which one would it be?

#AI #aimodels

result.title: OpenAI CHAOS Exposed In Leaked Texts At Trial
result.description:
Former OpenAI CTO Mira Murati's texts to Sam Altman when he was briefly fired from OpenAI was by far the biggest bombshell to come out of the Musk vs Altman trial so far.

For context: When Sam Altman was suddenly ousted from OpenAI in 2023 and rehired a few days later, the public story made it sound almost strategic and calculated.

These texts between Altman and Murati revealed the opposite. 

They showed all the confusion and panic happening behind the scenes and how Satya Nadella, Microsoft's CEO, was pulled in to basically mediate the whole mess.

All in all, not a good look for OpenAI or Sam, but we'll see what else this trial reveals as it continues on. 

And now it seems like "Directionally very bad" may be the new buzz phrase of the season 😂

#AI #ainews #technews #openai #chatgpt
